subject: Public Health
title: Air Pollution and Health
description: A short lesson to help you apply Air Pollution and Health.

hook.text: Air pollution kills 6.7 million people every year—making it the world's largest environmental health risk.

buildup.text: Fine particulate matter penetrates deep into lungs and enters the bloodstream—causing heart and lung disease.

discovery.text: Indoor air pollution from cooking fires kills 3.2 million annually—mostly women and children in poor households.

twist.text: 99% of the world's population breathes air exceeding WHO guidelines—almost no one is safe.

climax.text: Clean energy transitions, vehicle emission standards, and clean cookstoves can dramatically reduce exposure.

punchline.text: The air you breathe is the most basic health determinant.

quiz.question: How many people die from air pollution annually?
